response: Originating from 1960s U.S. defense research and the 1969 launch of ARPANET, the internet evolved through foundational technical milestones like the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP and Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web. Commercial and technological breakthroughs, including the 1990s dot-com boom, mid-2000s social media, the 2007 iPhone launch, and cloud computing, rapidly democratized access and expanded its global utility. Today, this decades-long transformation has produced a ubiquitous infrastructure that connects over 5 billion people and fundamentally underpins modern commerce, communication, and daily life.
